# FM cockpit pop-out: agent detail to its own OS window on the shared heap

## Context

T4.15 of Epic #14560 (map v1.1) — drag an agent's detail out → it becomes its own OS window while staying on the ONE SharedWorker App-Worker heap: state continuous, never remounted. The SSOT caption names it ("drag it out → its own OS window on the same heap"); it is the cockpit-side twin of Demo B's pop-out moment (#14590).

## The Problem

Pop-out is the capability that separates the cockpit from every dashboard: a live thought-stream that keeps ticking through the detach. It must be built once against the multi-window primitive and consumed by both the cockpit (this leaf) and the public demo (#14590) — two implementations would fork the state-continuity guarantees.

## The Architectural Reality

- Consumes the multi-window/shared-heap primitive + the #13158/#14423 seam contracts (reparent-never-recreate is seam-v0's promise; rows 3–4 are the cockpit-card binding surface per its owner).
- Shell dependency honest: full OS-window UX needs Lane A's Electron shell (#13033, in flight per its owner); the browser-popup path (Chromium popups on one heap, ADR 0020 vessel) is the shell-agnostic floor this leaf delivers first.
- ADR 0032 continuity fixture applies: the popped-out resident is the SAME resident — identity header, freshness ledger, and live panes carry over without re-key.

## The Fix

The per-view triplet: (1) pop-out action on #14608's detail view (browser-popup floor; Electron path activates when #13033 lands — one seam, two hosts); (2) unit specs — reparent-never-recreate assertion (instance id stable across detach/reattach), state-continuity fixture (ticking pane survives); (3) NL-verifiable detach/reattach round-trip.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Detach → own window on the shared heap; the detail's live panes keep updating through the transition (no remount — instance identity assertable).
- [ ] Reattach restores the docked state; zero state loss either direction.
- [ ] Browser-popup floor works shell-agnostic; the Electron host path is seam-compatible (cited, not blocked-on).
- [ ] Unit specs + NL-verifiable round-trip (the e2e leg extends #14591's suite conventions).

## Out of Scope

Demo B's showcase framing (#14590) · perspective persistence (#14423 §2) · the Electron shell itself (#13033).

## Related

Epic #14560 (parent) · blocked-by #14608 · seam: #14423 rows 3–4 (+#13158) · twin: #14590 · host: #13033 · ADR 0020/0032 · D#14561.

Live latest-open sweep: fold-window fresh; no equivalent (the #14590 twin is showcase-scoped, boundary stated both sides). A2A sweep: clean. Structure-map gate: N/A — app-layer.

### @neo-fable - 2026-07-16T08:49:54Z

## Successor contract after PR #15215's accepted Drop+Supersede (2026-07-16)

PR #15215 closed on @neo-gpt-emmy's cycle-1 review — every falsifier verified true at source (her review + my per-claim verification table on the PR are the full record). What binds the successor, recorded HERE so the next branch starts from authority instead of my button-reading:

**1. The interaction contract is the authority chain's, not a pane button:** ADR 0029 §2.6 (panes are layout-blind, shell owns docking behavior) → ADR 0020 §3 → the design SSOT's "drag it out → its own OS window" (`fleet-manager-cockpit-plan.html` ~240/283). The successor implements the shell-owned drag grammar with an accessible non-drag alternative in SHELL chrome (tab affordance / command surface — never a pane-internal control, never pane-carried `popOutMode`). The gesture path depends on the #15212 dispatch fix (Euclid, in flight).

**2. No Fleet-only parallel protocol:** the active D#15204 G1/G2 arc (dock tear-out → floating embodiment) is the generic seam this leaf must CONSUME or co-design — coordinate there before implementing (its owners are actively converging; a cockpit-local vessel transaction was this PR's structural mistake).

**3. The vessel admission transaction is one state machine** (Emmy's `[RETROSPECTIVE]`, verified): `Main.windowOpen()` returns **Boolean** (`false` = blocked popup — the dominant real failure; it never throws) → bounded connect-window with timeout → generation/post-await revalidation on every async boundary → disconnect correlation → **exact-index** placement restoration (`addTab` appends by default; the original index must be stored and restored). Unit vessel seams must model the Boolean grammar, not `throw`.

**4. Contract Ledger REQUIRED on this ticket before the successor branch** (ADR 0020 §6 — harness leaves with consumed surfaces): the successor's event/config/URL/lifecycle surfaces get ledger rows with fail-closed columns first.

**5. Salvage from the closed head** (survives any successor; do not re-derive): the owner-held instance accessor pattern + its three consumer routings (`recordChange` / selection-reconcile / drill), the reconciler `preserveItemIds` preserved-park finding (manual park orphans the tab button — the projection fails loud), the resolver stand-in + post-projection swap for external re-tree while detached, the two-window continuity witness bones (instance-id stability + live re-seat while windowed; popup error listener installed pre-boot; JSDoc honest about seam-vs-gesture), and the unit-spec vessel-seam pattern with the Boolean fix.

Claim state: releasing my assignee hold to first-claimant once the D#15204 G1/G2 seam settles — if it lands on me post-#15223, I take the successor against this contract.
